title: Sensex Crashes 1,400 Points as Middle East Tensions Escalate
summary: Indian equity markets witnessed a sharp sell-off on Monday as escalating geopolitical tensions between Iran and the US sent shockwaves across global financial markets. The BSE Sensex plunged over 1,400 points while the Nifty50 slipped below the 22,700 mark. Foreign institutional investors pulled out over ₹5,000 crore in a single session.
content:
Indian equity markets witnessed a sharp sell-off on Monday as escalating geopolitical tensions between Iran and the US sent shockwaves across global financial markets. The BSE Sensex plunged over 1,400 points while the Nifty50 slipped below the 22,700 mark.

Foreign institutional investors (FIIs) pulled out over ₹5,000 crore in a single session, marking the largest single-day outflow in 2026. The rupee also weakened to 86.45 against the US dollar.

"Markets are reacting to the uncertainty around the Strait of Hormuz situation," said Rajesh Kumar, Chief Market Strategist at ICICI Securities. "Until we see de-escalation, volatility will remain elevated."

All sectoral indices ended in the red, with the Nifty Metal and Nifty Energy indices bearing the brunt of the sell-off. IT stocks also faced pressure amid concerns about delayed Fed rate cuts due to rising crude oil prices.

Analysts recommend investors to remain cautious and avoid aggressive positions until geopolitical clarity emerges. Defensive sectors like FMCG and pharma may offer relative safety in the current environment.
author: Rajesh Mascarenhas

title: HDFC Bank MD Vows 'Ruthless' Fixing of Governance Issues
summary: HDFC Bank Managing Director Sashidhar Jagdishan has vowed to address governance concerns head-on, stating the bank will undertake a thorough review of all recent board decisions and ensure full transparency going forward.
content:
HDFC Bank Managing Director Sashidhar Jagdishan has vowed to address governance concerns head-on, stating the bank will undertake a thorough review of all recent board decisions.

"If you want to have good governance, you cannot be in a 'yes sir' mode," Jagdishan said in an exclusive interview. "We are going to be ruthless in fixing what needs to be fixed."

The bank plans to convene special board meetings to scrutinize previous resolutions. Any conduct-related issues will be tackled decisively. This comes after several shareholders raised concerns about the bank's governance framework post the HDFC Ltd merger.

Shares of HDFC Bank closed 2.3% lower at ₹1,542 on the BSE.
author: Saloni Shukla

title: Kotak Mahindra Bank Set to Acquire Deutsche Bank's Retail Business for ₹4,500 Crore
summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank is finalizing the acquisition of Deutsche Bank's India retail operations in a deal valued at approximately ₹4,500 crore, strengthening its retail lending capabilities.
content:
Kotak Mahindra Bank is poised to take a major leap by acquiring Deutsche Bank's retail operations in India for around ₹4,500 crore. This deal promises to strengthen Kotak's retail lending capabilities and broaden its base of deposits.

The acquisition includes Deutsche Bank's home loans, personal loans, and credit card portfolios, along with its network of retail branches across major metros.

"This is a transformative deal for Kotak's retail franchise," said an analyst from Motilal Oswal Securities. "It immediately adds scale and a high-quality customer base."

Deutsche Bank's exit reflects a larger global strategy to streamline operations and focus on corporate and investment banking.
author: MC Govardhana Rangan

title: Oil Surges Past $95 as Iran-US Tensions Threaten Hormuz Strait
summary: Brent crude surged past $95 per barrel as markets brace for potential disruption to oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz amid escalating military tensions between Iran and the United States.
content:
Brent crude oil prices surged past $95 per barrel on Monday, their highest level since October 2023, as escalating tensions between Iran and the United States raised fears of supply disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz.

The strait handles roughly 20% of global oil trade. Trump's deadline for Iran to cease its nuclear activities expired today, raising the specter of military action.

"A closure or even partial disruption of the Strait would be catastrophic for global energy markets," said Vandana Hari, CEO of Vanda Insights. "Asia, which imports the bulk of its oil through this chokepoint, would be worst affected."

Indian oil marketing companies are already exploring alternative supply routes and discussing strategic petroleum reserve releases.
author: Deepshikha Sikarwar

title: Silver Crashes ₹14,000/kg, Gold Tanks ₹7,000 on War Fears
summary: Precious metals witnessed extreme volatility as silver crashed ₹14,000 per kg and gold fell ₹7,000 per 10 grams amid rising oil prices and inflation worries triggered by the Iran conflict.
content:
Silver prices crashed by ₹14,000 per kg on the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX), while gold tumbled ₹7,000 per 10 grams in one of the sharpest single-day corrections in recent memory.

The selloff was driven by a flight to cash as rising crude oil prices stoked inflation fears, leading traders to liquidate positions across asset classes.

"Investors are de-risking across the board," said Kishore Narne, head of commodities at Motilal Oswal Financial Services. "The simultaneous crash in gold and silver is unusual and reflects extreme risk aversion."

Analysts suggest waiting for price stabilization before making fresh commitments in precious metals.
author: Ram Sahgal

title: Asia's Industrial Revolution is Switching Off Gas for Solar Power
summary: Energy-hungry industries in Pakistan and India are rapidly shifting to solar power, reducing reliance on expensive imported LNG and insulating themselves from geopolitical disruptions.
content:
Energy-hungry industries across South Asia are making a dramatic shift from imported liquefied natural gas to solar power, driven by economics and energy security concerns.

In India, over 40 GW of rooftop and open-access solar capacity has been installed by industrial consumers, reducing their power costs by up to 60%. The ongoing Iran conflict has only accelerated this transition.

"Solar is no longer an environmental choice — it's a business imperative," said Vinay Rustagi, Managing Director of Bridge to India. "Industries that haven't switched are paying 3-4x more for energy."

Meanwhile, Bangladesh's slower adoption of renewables leaves it vulnerable to supply shortages and rising energy costs.
author: Sarita C Singh

title: India's GDP Growth Forecast Cut to 6.2% Amid Global Headwinds
summary: The RBI has revised India's GDP growth forecast for FY27 down to 6.2% from 6.5%, citing elevated crude oil prices and global uncertainty as key risks to the economic outlook.
content:
The Reserve Bank of India has revised its GDP growth projection for FY27 downward to 6.2% from the earlier estimate of 6.5%, citing elevated crude oil prices, geopolitical uncertainties, and their potential impact on inflation and fiscal balances.

RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra noted that while domestic fundamentals remain strong, the external environment poses significant challenges. "We need to be prepared for a scenario where oil stays above $90 for an extended period," he said.

The central bank also flagged risks to the current account deficit, which could widen to 2.5% of GDP if oil prices sustain at current levels.

Economists at Goldman Sachs have also trimmed their India growth forecast to 6.0% for the current fiscal year.
author: Deepshikha Sikarwar

title: Wipro Bags $1.2 Billion Deal from European Automaker
summary: Wipro has won a mega $1.2 billion IT outsourcing contract from a leading European automobile manufacturer, one of the largest deals in the Indian IT sector this year.
content:
Wipro Limited has secured a landmark $1.2 billion IT outsourcing deal from a major European automobile manufacturer, marking one of the largest contract wins in the Indian IT industry this fiscal year.

The deal spans seven years and covers digital transformation, cloud migration, and AI-driven manufacturing solutions. Wipro will deploy over 3,000 engineers across Europe and India.

"This win validates our strategy of investing deeply in industry-specific solutions," said Srinivas Pallia, CEO of Wipro. "The automotive sector is ripe for digital disruption, and we're well-positioned to lead it."

Wipro shares rose 4.5% intraday following the announcement.
author: Surabhi Agarwal

title: Mutual Fund SIP Flows Touch Record ₹25,000 Crore in February
summary: Monthly SIP contributions to mutual funds have hit an all-time high of ₹25,000 crore in February 2026, reflecting deepening retail investor participation despite market volatility.
content:
Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) contributions to Indian mutual funds touched a record ₹25,000 crore in February 2026, data from the Association of Mutual Funds in India (AMFI) showed.

The milestone reflects the growing maturity of Indian retail investors who continue to invest through market downturns. The number of active SIP accounts has crossed 10 crore for the first time.

"SIP flows remaining robust despite market corrections shows that investor behavior has fundamentally shifted," said Nilesh Shah, Managing Director of Kotak AMC.

Small-cap and mid-cap funds continue to attract the highest SIP allocations, though some fund houses have imposed restrictions on lump-sum investments in these categories.
author: Prashant Mahesh

title: Zomato to Launch 10-Minute Medicine Delivery Across 15 Cities
summary: Zomato is expanding into quick-commerce pharma with a 10-minute medicine delivery service, starting with 15 cities. The move puts it in direct competition with PharmEasy and 1mg.
content:
Zomato has announced plans to launch 10-minute medicine delivery across 15 Indian cities by April 2026, leveraging its Blinkit dark store network.

The service will initially cover OTC medicines and health supplements, with plans to add prescription drugs after obtaining necessary regulatory approvals.

"Healthcare access should be as fast as food delivery," said Deepinder Goyal, CEO of Zomato. "We're building the infrastructure to make that possible."

The move intensifies competition in the quick-commerce pharmaceutical space, where Tata's 1mg and PharmEasy already have significant presence.
author: Ananya Bhattacharya

title: 8th Pay Commission: Government Likely to Announce Implementation Date Soon
summary: The Centre is expected to announce the implementation date for the 8th Pay Commission recommendations soon, with employee bodies pushing for backdated revisions from January 2026.
content:
The central government is expected to announce the implementation timeline for the 8th Pay Commission within the next few weeks, according to sources familiar with the matter.

Employee bodies including AITUC have written to the government demanding that salary and pension revisions be backdated to January 1, 2026. The commission's recommendations are expected to benefit over 50 lakh central government employees and 65 lakh pensioners.

A fitment factor of 2.57 is being discussed, which would raise the minimum basic pay from ₹18,000 to ₹46,260.

"We are hopeful that the government will consider our demands favorably," said an AITUC representative.
author: Yogima Sharma

title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Real-Time Research Capabilities
summary: OpenAI has unveiled GPT-5, featuring real-time web research, multi-modal reasoning, and a new 'deep thinking' mode that can solve complex problems step by step.
content:
OpenAI has officially launched GPT-5, its most advanced AI model to date, featuring real-time web research capabilities, enhanced multi-modal reasoning, and a revolutionary 'deep thinking' mode.

The new model can browse the internet in real-time, synthesize information from multiple sources, and provide sourced answers with citations. The deep thinking mode allows it to break down complex problems into steps and reason through them methodically.

"GPT-5 represents a fundamental shift from language models to reasoning engines," said Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI. "It doesn't just generate text — it thinks."

Indian tech companies are already exploring enterprise applications, with TCS and Infosys announcing integration partnerships.
author: Surabhi Agarwal

title: Assembly Elections 2026: EC Announces Dates for 5 States
summary: The Election Commission has announced polling dates for West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam, and Puducherry. Voting will be held in April with results on May 4.
content:
The Election Commission of India has announced the schedule for assembly elections in five states — West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam, and Puducherry.

West Bengal and Assam will vote in multiple phases starting April 5, while Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and Puducherry will have single-phase polling on April 23. Results for all five states will be declared on May 4.

The elections are seen as a crucial mid-term test for the ruling BJP and opposition parties ahead of the 2029 general elections.

"These elections will reshape the political landscape of regional politics," said political analyst Prashant Kishor.
author: Pranab Dhal Samanta

title: Reliance Jio Launches AI-Powered JioTranslate for Real-Time Communication
summary: Reliance Jio has launched JioTranslate, an AI-powered real-time translation service supporting 22 Indian languages, aimed at breaking language barriers across business and personal communication.
content:
Reliance Jio has unveiled JioTranslate, an AI-powered real-time translation service that supports all 22 scheduled Indian languages plus English.

The service uses Jio's proprietary large language model trained specifically on Indian language data and can translate voice calls, text messages, and video conferences in real-time.

"India's diversity is its strength, but language barriers have limited economic participation," said Akash Ambani, Chairman of Jio. "JioTranslate will democratize communication across India."

The service is available free for Jio subscribers and at a nominal fee for others. Early trials show 94% accuracy across major language pairs.
author: Gulveen Aulakh

title: Delhi Power Tariffs Set to Rise in April After Decade-Long Freeze
summary: Delhi electricity rates may increase from April as the AAP government prepares to clear ₹38,000 crore in pending dues to power distribution companies after a Supreme Court directive.
content:
Electricity rates in Delhi are expected to increase from April 2026, ending a decade-long tariff freeze under the AAP government.

The Delhi government plans to pay over ₹38,000 crore in pending regulatory assets to three power discoms — BSES Rajdhani, BSES Yamuna, and Tata Power Delhi Distribution. This follows a Supreme Court directive mandating clearance of accumulated dues.

While the government intends to provide subsidies to lessen the impact on consumers, analysts estimate that tariffs could rise by 15-20% for commercial and industrial users.

"This was long overdue. The financial health of discoms was deteriorating rapidly," said an energy sector analyst.
author: Rakesh Raman

title: PhonePe Crosses 1 Billion Monthly Transactions Milestone
summary: PhonePe has become the first Indian fintech to cross 1 billion monthly transactions, commanding over 48% of UPI transaction volume as digital payments penetrate deeper into India.
content:
PhonePe has achieved a historic milestone by crossing 1 billion monthly transactions on the Unified Payments Interface (UPI), becoming the first Indian fintech platform to reach this landmark.

The Walmart-backed company now commands over 48% of all UPI transactions by volume. The milestone was driven by rapid adoption in tier-2 and tier-3 cities, where PhonePe has emerged as the dominant payments platform.

"We're just getting started," said Sameer Nigam, CEO of PhonePe. "The next billion transactions will come from insurance, mutual funds, and lending — not just payments."

PhonePe is expected to file for an IPO in 2026, with an estimated valuation of $15 billion.
author: Samidha Sharma

title: Tata Motors Reports Record EV Sales; Nexon EV Dominates Market
summary: Tata Motors has reported record electric vehicle sales of 22,000 units in February, with the Nexon EV maintaining its market leadership position with a 62% share of India's EV passenger car market.
content:
Tata Motors continues to dominate India's electric vehicle market, reporting record sales of 22,000 EV units in February 2026. The Nexon EV maintained its commanding 62% market share in the passenger EV segment.

The company also launched the Curvv EV, which has received over 50,000 bookings within its first month. Tata plans to launch two more EV models by the end of FY27.

"EVs are no longer a niche — they're mainstream," said Shailesh Chandra, MD of Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles. "We aim for EVs to constitute 25% of our total sales by FY28."

The Indian EV market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 45% over the next five years.
author: Nabeel A Khan

title: RBI Keeps Repo Rate Unchanged at 6.25% Amid Inflation Concerns
summary: The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee voted 4-2 to hold the repo rate at 6.25%, citing elevated food inflation and crude oil price risks despite moderating core inflation.
content:
The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.25% in its March 2026 policy review, voting 4-2 in favor of the status quo.

RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra highlighted that while core inflation has moderated to 4.1%, food inflation remains elevated at 8.2%. Rising crude oil prices pose an additional upside risk to the inflation trajectory.

"The MPC decided to wait for greater clarity on the inflation outlook before considering any further rate adjustments," Malhotra said in his policy statement.

Markets had been pricing in a 25 bps cut, and the pause led to a brief sell-off in bond markets before recovering.
author: Gayatri Nayak

title: India's Startup Funding Rebounds: $3.2 Billion Raised in Q1 2026
summary: Indian startups raised $3.2 billion in Q1 2026, marking a 45% year-on-year increase and signaling a strong recovery from the funding winter that gripped the ecosystem in 2023-24.
content:
Indian startups have raised $3.2 billion in the first quarter of 2026, a robust 45% increase compared to the same period last year, according to data from Tracxn.

The recovery is being led by AI/ML startups, which attracted $890 million across 42 deals. Fintech and SaaS continue to be top-funded sectors.

Notable deals include Ola Electric's $500 million Series F and Meesho's $350 million round. Early-stage funding also showed strong momentum with seed and pre-Series A deals up 60%.

"The funding winter is definitively over," said Rajan Anandan, Managing Director of Sequoia Capital India. "We're seeing a new generation of founders building for AI-first markets."
author: Samidha Sharma

title: LPG Cylinder May Get Lighter as OMCs Explore Options Amid Iran Crisis
summary: Oil marketing companies are weighing the option of introducing lighter LPG cylinders to manage supply constraints arising from disruptions in Iranian oil and gas imports.
content:
Indian oil marketing companies (OMCs) are evaluating the introduction of lighter LPG cylinders as an option to manage supply constraints caused by the ongoing Iran crisis.

Currently, domestic LPG cylinders contain 14.2 kg of gas. OMCs are considering reducing this to 12 kg or offering parallel 5 kg cylinders at subsidized rates to stretch available supply.

"We are looking at all options to ensure uninterrupted supply to households," said a senior official at Indian Oil Corporation. "A lighter cylinder is one possibility being discussed."

India imports about 60% of its LPG requirements, with Iran being a significant supplier. The disruption has also led to increased imports from Argentina and the UAE.
author: Sarita C Singh
